zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard於1521年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾維根時，她的父親Thomas Gerard56歲，母親Maud Bold40歲。她於1535年與Thomas Robert Goodloe在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿斯普爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1597年6月1日在英國英格蘭藍卡爾阿斯普爾享年76歲，埋葬在英國藍卡爾阿斯普爾。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard于1521年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾維根时，她的父亲Thomas Gerard56岁，母亲Maud Bold40岁。她于1535年与Thomas Robert Goodloe在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿斯普爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1597年6月1日在英国英格兰藍卡爾阿斯普爾享年76岁，埋葬在英国藍卡爾阿斯普爾。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gerard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Jean-Pierre, Murielle, Emile, Pascal, Pierre, Agathe, Alain, Aldes, Antoine, Armand.

French (Gérard), Walloon (mainly Gérard), and English (of Norman origin): from the personal name Gérard, Gerard (from Old French Gerart, Girart), composed of the ancient Germanic elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ + hard ‘hardy, brave, strong’. As a surname of French origin it is also found in Haiti. Compare Garrard and also French Guerard , English Garard , Gerrard , and Jarrard .

variants.name: Gerardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Enzo, Gaspare, Luigi, Rocco, Antonio, Canio, Carmine, Filippo, Francesco, Giovanni, Libero.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the ancient Germanic personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ). This surname is also found in southern France.
German and French (mainly Nord): Latinized (humanistic) patronymic from the personal name German Gerhard , French Gérard (see Gerard ), using the genitive of the Latinized form Gerardus.

variants.name: Garrison
meanings: English: from a shortened form of Middle English Gerardson, a patronymic from the personal name Gerard + son, or an Anglicized form of its Dutch cognate Gerritsen . The English surname is probably in most cases of Flemish or Dutch origin. It was brought to Britain by immigrants from the Low Countries, especially in the 15th and 16th centuries.
Americanized form of Jewish   Gerson   (see   Gershon  ).
Americanized form of Dutch   Gerritsen  .

variants.name: Garrard
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Gerard  .
Altered form of French Gérard (see Gerard ).
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Geenen
meanings: Dutch, Flemish, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Geen, a pet form of Gerard .

variants.name: Gerardo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Alberto, Alfredo, Aurelio, Carlos, Jesus, Juan, Norberto, Ramon, Tomas, Ana.  Italian Antonio, Carmine, Dino, Enrico, Marco, Marco Antonio.
Spanish and Italian: from the personal name Gerardo (see Gerard ).

variants.name: Gerald
meanings: English (of Norman origin); French (Gérald): from the ancient Germanic personal name Gerald, Girald, composed of the elements gēr ‘spear, lance’ and wald ‘power, authority’. It was introduced to Britain by the Normans and was fairly common, but less so than Gerard , with which it was early confused. In France the much more common variants of this surname are Géraud and Giraud .

variants.name: Gerrard
meanings: English: variant of   Gerard  .

variants.name: Geron
meanings: French (Géron): from the personal name Gero, Gerone, a short form of ancient Germanic names formed with the element gār, gēr ‘spear, lance’, as for example Gérard (see Gerard ).
Altered form of French Guérin (see Guerin ).
English (Lancashire): of Norman origin, from the personal name Geron of ancient Germanic origin, a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Gherardi
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Silvio, Alessandro, Angelo, Antonio, Donato, Egidio, Ennio, Enzo, Gildo, Lucio, Luigi, Primo.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of Gherardo, one of themany Italian equivalents of Gerard .

variants.name: Garard
meanings: English (London and Buckinghamshire): variant of   Gerard  .

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabetha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard was born in 1521, in Wigan, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Gerard, was 56 and her mother, Maud Bold, was 40. She married Thomas Robert Goodloe in 1535, in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 1 June 1597, in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 76, and was buried in Aspull, Lancashire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard est née en 1521, à Wigan, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Gerard, avait 56 ans et sa mère, Maud Bold, avait 40 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Robert Goodloe en 1535, à Aspull, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 1 juin 1597, à Aspull, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterrée à Aspull, Lancashire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard 1521 in Wigan, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thomas Gerard 56 und ihre Mutter Maud Bold 40. Sie hat 1535 in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom Thomas Robert Goodloe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 1. Juni 1597 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Aspull, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Aspull, Lancashire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard è nata il nel 1521 nel Wigan, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Gerard, aveva 56 anni e sua madre, Maud Bold, aveva 40 anni. Lei sposato Thomas Robert Goodloe nel 1535, nel Aspull, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 1 giugno 1597, nel Aspull, Lancashire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolta a Aspull, Lancashire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerardが1521年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアウィガンで生まれた時，父Thomas Gerardは56歳，母Maud Boldは40歳であった。1535年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアアスプウルでThomas Robert Goodloeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1597年6月1日にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアアスプウルで76歳で死去し，イギリスランカシュアイアアスプウルにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard(이)가 1521년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 위건에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Gerard(은)는 56세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud Bold(은)는 40세였다. 그녀는 1535년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 아스풀에서 Thomas Robert Goodloe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1597년 6월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 아스풀에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 랭카실르 아스풀에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard nasceu em 1521, em Wigan, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Gerard, tinha 56 anos e sua mãe, Maud Bold, tinha 40 anos. Ela casou-se com Thomas Robert Goodloe em 1535, em Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1 de junho de 1597, em Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 76 anos, e foi sepultada em Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard родилась в 1521 г. в местности виган, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Gerard было 56 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud Bold, 40. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Robert Goodloe в 1535 г. в местности аспулл, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 1 июня 1597 г. в местности аспулл, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 76 лет и была похоронена в местности аспулл, ланкашир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Anna Elizabetha de Gerard nació en 1521, en Wigan, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Gerard, tenía 56 años y su mamá, Maud Bold, tenía 40 años. Se casó con Thomas Robert Goodloe en 1535, en Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 1 de junio de 1597, en Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultada en Aspull, Lancashire, Inglaterra.
